summary: Matched two small functions in `melee/lb/lb_0192.c`, raising the file report to 10.15% matched: `lb_80019230`, a DVD drive-status classifier implemented as a switch over `DVDGetDriveStatus`, and `lb_800195D0`, a wrapper that calls `lb_800192A8(lb_8001955C)` then `lb_8001CC84()`. The PR also renamed the adjacent `lb_0198` card-game unit to `lbcardgame`, moved/added the related header declarations, updated build object lists and split ranges, and adjusted symbol/data metadata around the `lb_0192`/`lbcardgame` boundary.
what_changed: Added C source for `lb_80019230`, mapping `DVDGetDriveStatus()` return values to local enum-like results: status `5 -> 0`, `4 -> 1`, `6 -> 2`, `11 -> 3`, `-1 -> 4`, `1 -> 5`, and default `-1`.
Added C source for `lb_800195D0`, which calls `lb_800192A8(lb_8001955C)` and then `lb_8001CC84()`.
Expanded `src/melee/lb/lb_0192.h` with address-commented declarations for `lb_800192A8`, `lb_8001955C`, and `lb_800195D0`, and switched its include guard to address-style `GALE01_019230`.
Renamed `src/melee/lb/lb_0198.c` to `src/melee/lb/lbcardgame.c` with 100% similarity and replaced the old `lb_0198.h` with `lbcardgame.h`.
Updated `configure.py` and `obj_files.mk` to refer to `melee/lb/lbcardgame.c` / `asm/melee/lb/lbcardgame.s` instead of `lb_0198`.
Updated split ranges so `lb_0192.c` data extends to `0x803BAAE0`, while `lbcardgame.c` starts at `0x803BAAE0` instead of `0x803BAAA8`.
Updated `symbols.txt`, including renaming `fn_8001955C` to `lb_8001955C` and refining several data objects near `0x803BAB40` through `0x803BACB0` as strings, local data, or resized objects.
Updated scratches for `func_80019230` and `func_800195D0` from `100%` to `OK`, reflecting that those scratches were incorporated.
Adjusted `lbdvd.s` string/gap representation around `lbDvd_804D37E4` and `lbDvd_804D37EC`, changing explicit embedded null padding into separate hidden gap objects.
smart_moves: The DVD status helper was reduced to a direct switch over SDK status values, which naturally corresponds to the original jump-table shape seen at `jumptable_803BAAA8`.

The PR did not only add two matched functions; it also cleaned up the neighboring file identity by renaming generic `lb_0198` to the more meaningful `lbcardgame`, then updated includes, build config, and split metadata together.
The wrapper `lb_800195D0` reused existing unresolved functions by adding only the needed prototypes, avoiding over-decompiling surrounding logic without evidence.
Renaming `fn_8001955C` to `lb_8001955C` made the callback usable from C and documented its address in the header.
The split boundary adjustment preserved the data ownership boundary after `lb_0192` absorbed the jump table/data up to `0x803BAAE0`.
decomp_lessons: When a small helper is just a status remap, a C `switch` can match a PPC jump table cleanly if case values mirror the SDK constants and the default path returns the same sentinel.
Matching even tiny functions may require adjacent-file cleanup: split ranges, symbols, object lists, and includes all need to agree with the new source boundary.
File renames from address-placeholder names to semantic names should be accompanied by header migration and downstream include updates in consumers such as `gmmain.c`.
Scratch entries marked `100%` should be changed to `OK` once incorporated into the main tree, preserving provenance while indicating completion.
Data string sizes and padding matter for split correctness; explicit null padding in assembly can be converted into separate hidden gap symbols when symbol sizes are refined.
naming_conventions: Address-based include guards used here: `GALE01_019230` for `lb_0192.h` and `GALE01_019880` for `lbcardgame.h`.
Address comments precede declarations in headers, e.g. `/* 0192A8 */ void lb_800192A8(UNK_T cb);`.
The generic file name `lb_0198` was replaced by the semantic module name `lbcardgame`.
The previously anonymous/internal-looking symbol `fn_8001955C` was renamed to library-style `lb_8001955C`.
Unknown callback/function signatures remained conservative with `UNK_T`, `UNK_RET`, and `UNK_PARAMS` rather than inventing precise types.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched `lb_80019230` as a switch over `DVDGetDriveStatus`, producing the same status classification and default `-1` behavior.
The asm diff shows conversion from raw labels such as `lb_80019264` to local labels like `.L_80019264` under `.fn lb_80019230`, consistent with jump-table case labels not being global functions.
The jump table was named/refined as `jumptable_803BAAA8` and kept with the `lb_0192` data range.
The generated assembly used `.fn`/`.endfn`, `.text`, `.balign 4`, and hex immediates in the refreshed assembly listing.
Data split repair moved the `lbcardgame` data start from `0x803BAAA8` to `0x803BAAE0`, preventing the `lb_0192` jump table/data from being attributed to the renamed card-game unit.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue matching `lb_800192A8`, `fn_800195FC`, or `lb_80019628` in `lb_0192.c`.
Check later work on `lbcardgame.c` for whether `UNK_T` and `UNK_PARAMS` declarations in `lbcardgame.h` or `lb_0192.h` were refined.
Look for additional split-boundary fixes around `0x803BAAA8` and `0x803BAAE0` if data ownership issues recur.
Search for other PRs converting `lb_019x` placeholder filenames into semantic `lb*` module names.
